Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant 690 Market Club Owners Associations Notice Of Joinder In Marriotts Motion To Appoint General Judicial Referee
Off Calendar Mar-26-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT 690 MARKET CLUB OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Joinder In Marriott's Motion To Appoint General Judicial Referee, the motion for reference is granted and this case is stayed. CCP 638 is a legislatively prescribed procedure where parties may waive their right to a jury trial. In Grafton Partners, L.P. v. Sup. Crt. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 944, 959-961, the court explains that CCP 631 has no bearing on the validity of a contractual reference provision. The court has discretion to deny reference, but cannot do so arbitrarily. There is no unconscionability and the referee can fairly apportion the fees. The court denies the fee request. The prevailing party to the "litigation" is entitled to fees under the contract. This motion is merely one aspect of the litigation. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
